title: Migration, Recovery, Longitudinal Validation, and Advanced Defense · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for migration, recovery, longitudinal validation, and advanced defense contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in advanced product transition and defense packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.
Define recovery point and time needs, backup scope, integrity, encryption, access, retention, clean restore, and application reconciliation.
Measure task outcomes, guardrails, adoption, abandonment, support, accessibility, reliability, and unintended effects over an appropriate period.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy
steps.instruction:
debug case — A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.

Inspect the Migration Strategy trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy, keep Migration Strategy connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Migration Strategy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Migration Strategy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Migration Strategy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Big-bang migration is simplest because it avoids dual operation.

stimulus.title: Migration Strategy case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Big-bang migration is simplest because it avoids dual operation.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk. Artifact under review: advanced product transition and defense packet.

options.text: The Migration Strategy record says advanced product transition and defense packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Migration Strategy.

For inspect the failure record, apply Migration Strategy without its stated preconditions, omit the advanced product transition and defense packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Migration Strategy for advanced product transition and defense packet.
The record names the assumption, applies Migration Strategy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Advanced Retrospective structural check is only one gate. advanced product transition and defense packet must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Advanced Retrospective
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Advanced Retrospective: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A big-bang cutover relies on one launch-day study and a backup job that has never produced a clean, reconciled restore. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Evaluate product, process, leadership, ethics, operations, and learning against prior predictions and define a harder transfer challenge.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Advanced Retrospective.
steps.why: Retrieving Advanced Retrospective through the concrete advanced product transition and defense packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Advanced Retrospective, keep Advanced Retrospective connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate product, process, leadership, ethics, operations, and learning against prior predictions and define a harder transfer challenge.
